title: Damapana blanda
taxonomy: Damapana blanda is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. The classification of Damapana blanda is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Sapindales. This plant is also known by other names, but its classification remains consistent across botanical resources. Its classification is used to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Sapindaceae family.

intro: Damapana blanda, also known as Madhuca blanda, is a tropical tree species native to the forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The health benefits of Damapana blanda include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Damapana blanda is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, with the latter being used topically to treat skin conditions, and the former being consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
